title: Tephroseris salisburgensis
intro: Tephroseris salisburgensis, also known as Salzburger Thalassia, is a rare and endangered alpine plant native to the Alps. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Tephroseris salisburgensis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability is limited due to its restricted habitat and conservation status.
taxonomy: Tephroseris salisburgensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the Austrian Alps and is classified as a perennial herb. The classification of Tephroseris salisburgensis is still under review, but it is generally placed within the genus Tephroseris. This genus is sometimes considered a synonym of Seriphium, adding to the complexity of its classification. Tephroseris salisburgensis is closely related to other species within the Asteraceae family, which is one of the largest and most diverse plant families.
